title: Network Media, Devices, PoE, and Diagnostic Tools · Independent Support Trial
summary: A different user, device, environment, symptom, constraint, and changed condition requires an independent transfer of network media, devices, poe, and diagnostic tools into a new physical and logical network path inventory. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of physical and logical network path inventory with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Match twisted pair, coaxial, fiber, connector, category, wavelength, distance, bandwidth, interference, bend, grounding, and environment to requirements.
Distinguish modem, ONT, router, switch, access point, firewall, bridge, repeater, controller, load balancer, proxy, NAS, and IoT hub roles.
Reason about source and powered-device standards, negotiation, class, per-port and total budget, cable loss, distance, fallback, and injector placement.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# comptia-a-plus cumulative fictional support evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic text-contract review only. They never execute learner
# commands, inspect the host, touch devices or accounts, connect to networks, open equipment,
# handle malware, erase media, contact people, or claim certification.
workspace: support
ticket-id: fictional-ticket
user-impact: [replace with user outcome, scope, severity, and urgency]
environment: [replace with asset, version, configuration, owner, dependency, and evidence time]
safety-authority: [replace with consent, hazards, data custody, privacy, access, and stop rules]
baseline: [replace with expected behavior, known-good state, conditions, and measurement]
symptom: [replace with observed behavior, reproduction, frequency, timing, and evidence]
recent-change: [replace with timeline, relevant changes, counterevidence, and unknowns]
hypothesis: [replace with falsifiable cause, competing explanation, prediction, and confidence]
test: [replace with one safe reversible discriminating test, changed case, and stop rule]
observation: [replace with actual result, provenance, rejected inference, and next decision]
repair-rollback: [replace with least-change repair, authority, backup, trigger, and restored state]
verify: [replace with user outcome, original, changed, side-effect, restart, and recurrence checks]
prevention: [replace with prevention, monitoring, owner, review date, and residual risk]
escalation: [replace with stop threshold, destination, evidence packet, and requested decision]
documentation: [replace with plain structured status, redaction, access, handoff, and uncertainty]
transfer-boundary: authorized disposable equipment or simulator only
review:

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Lab Media Selection
steps.instruction:
lab case — An office combines mislabeled copper, fiber, PoE, switches, injectors, and access points with intermittent link and power evidence. The lab variation 4 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: defend one safe causal support decision

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Lab Media Selection.

content.text: An office combines mislabeled copper, fiber, PoE, switches, injectors, and access points with intermittent link and power evidence. steps.instruction:
lab case — An office combines mislabeled copper, fiber, PoE, switches, injectors, and access points with intermittent link and power evidence. The lab variation 2 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Create a port, jack, patch, cable, device, power, address, VLAN, owner, and evidence map that separates labels from observed state.

One precondition fails while output still looks plausible. What is the correct response?

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Test an acceptance boundary · Lab Path Documentation.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for create a port, jack, patch, cable, device, power, address, vlan, owner, and evidence map that separates labels from observed state. For CompTIA A+ support Test an acceptance boundary · Lab Path Documentation, keep Path Documentation connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Fault-isolation decision
content.text: Select evidence that identifies a cause, not a patch that only hides the Path Documentation symptom in physical and logical network path inventory during this lab case.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: CompTIA A+ Core 1 220-1201 Exam Objectives — Controls every current Core 1 mobile, networking, hardware, virtualization, cloud, and troubleshooting objective and weighting.

stimulus.title: Path Documentation failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: An old network diagram should be trusted until a change ticket explicitly disproves it.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Create a port, jack, patch, cable, device, power, address, VLAN, owner, and evidence map that separates labels from observed state.

options.text: Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Path Documentation condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

Keep the plausible Path Documentation output and hide the violated physical and logical network path inventory assumption instead of explaining the failure. That claim fails the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary for Path Documentation.

For test an acceptance boundary, apply one familiar repair, omit user impact, authorization, safety, asset and version identity, baseline, recent change, competing cause, prediction, observation, rollback, changed-case verification, accessibility, privacy, escalation, and controlled transfer, then accept a missing symptom as support proof. This does not satisfy Path Documentation for physical and logical network path inventory.

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Defend the transferred model · Lab Path Documentation
steps.instruction:
Write the lab evidence note for Path Documentation: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — An office combines mislabeled copper, fiber, PoE, switches, injectors, and access points with intermittent link and power evidence. content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Create a port, jack, patch, cable, device, power, address, VLAN, owner, and evidence map that separates labels from observed state.
